notes: Works quite well. The Source engine utilizes d3d9 but runs well enough to keep even a consistent 144fps at 1080p at maximum settings. The only issue is the font kerning which is a known issue with Source games run under Wine. There is no known workaround. The font will be slightly less readable, but still legible.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: Didn't work on previous Proton versions, now seems to work flawlessly – game loads and runs flawlessly (250+ FPS on highest settings on 1920x1080!), at least on the first level, and server browser seems to load fine as well.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: Didn't work on 4.1-3. Had to manually select a lower version of Proton. Works fine although menu font looks a bit broken.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: Font rendering is sometimes off, but mostly fine, also binding items to the quick access menu doesn't seem to stick and I don't remember having that issue on Windows. Otherwise perfectly playable.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: Intro video renders in the top-left of screen at a fixed resolution, as should the menu, only instead of a menu it renders complete garbage, sometimes meaningless static, and sometimes snippets of framebuffer data still in memory from a previous game.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: The game will not run on the latest version of Proton, but this can easily be corrected by downgrading your Proton version to 3.16-9 by forcing the game to run with a specific version of Proton under Properties>General. After doing that, the game runs like native and works flawlessly. Multiplayer runs great, but I couldn't get a non-LAN server to work properly, even after opening up all of the required ports. If you have similar issues and are looking to play without being limited to LAN network members then you can use something like Hamachi using one their available Linux interfaces.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: Had to adjust screen size and resolution through the "~" menu, as default only shows like 25% of the game.  After that, works just fine without any necessary tweaks or adjustments.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy

notes: With my specs and drivers the game just works. Performance is adequate - 60 fps all the way.
title: E.Y.E: Divine Cybermancy
